Upstream:  supranational/blst
Origin:    https://github.com/supranational/blst
Tag:       v0.3.14
Commit:    8c7db7fe8d2ce6e76dc398ebd4d475c0ec564355
License:   Apache-2.0 (see LICENSE in this directory)

Provides BLS12-381 G1/G2 group arithmetic, pairings, hash-to-curve and
serialization for the Plutus bls12_381_* builtins (added in a later task).
This task only vendors and wires the library plus a smoke test; the builtin
bodies are implemented separately.

Build configuration: portable, no-assembly build
  The library is compiled as a single translation unit, src/server.c, which
  #includes the implementation .c files it needs:
    keygen.c hash_to_field.c e1.c map_to_g1.c e2.c map_to_g2.c fp12_tower.c
    pairing.c aggregate.c exp.c sqrt.c recip.c bulk_addition.c multi_scalar.c
    consts.c vect.c exports.c rb_tree.c cpuid.c
  (pentaroot.c is included only under BLST_FR_PENTAROOT, which is never
  defined here.)

  Two compile defines are set on the isolated CMake OBJECT target
  (see lib/CMakeLists.txt):
    __BLST_NO_ASM__   - select the portable reference C field arithmetic in
                        src/no_asm.h instead of any assembly. With this define
                        there is no assembly anywhere, so the library builds on
                        WASM/Emscripten and the armv6/armv7 cross builds.
    __BLST_PORTABLE__ - also disable the runtime-dispatched SHA-256 assembly
                        kernels (blst_sha256_block_data_order_shaext /
                        blst_sha256_block_armv8 in src/sha256.h), falling back
                        to the pure-C blst_sha256_block_data_order. Without this
                        define, on an x86_64 host built with -msha or on Apple
                        Silicon (which defines __ARM_FEATURE_CRYPTO) the header
                        would reference an assembly SHA-256 symbol that the
                        no-asm build does not provide, and the link would fail.

Local change to the vendored source (recorded per the vendoring rules):
  src/vect.h - the limb-size selection block is reordered so that the
    __BLST_NO_ASM__/__wasm64__ branch (32-bit limbs, the path no_asm.h is
    written for) is tested before the __x86_64__/__aarch64__ branch (64-bit
    limbs). Upstream only ships the no-assembly path for architectures other
    than x86_64/aarch64, so on those two it would otherwise pick 64-bit limbs
    and fail to compile no_asm.h (which defines its 64-bit double-limb llimb_t
    only for the 32-bit-limb case). Reordering lets the portable build compile
    on x86_64 and aarch64 too. No copyright or license text was altered.

Removed from the upstream tree to keep this lean (no copyright text altered):
  - src/asm/        - all .pl assembly generators (not used in the no-asm build).
  - build/          - assembly.S and the per-platform object/refresh tooling
                      (only used by the assembly build).
  - bindings/blst_t.hpp, bindings/blst.hpp, bindings/blst.swg - C++/SWIG glue.
  - All language bindings: bindings/{go,rust,python,node.js,java,c#,emscripten}
    and bindings/vectors.
  - src/client_min_pk.c, src/client_min_sig.c - alternative amalgamation units
    (server.c is the unit we compile; these are not #included by it).
  - build.sh, build.bat, go.mod, README.md, SECURITY.md, the logo image.

Vendored files:
  LICENSE                 - upstream Apache-2.0 license, copied verbatim.
  bindings/blst.h         - public C API.
  bindings/blst_aux.h     - auxiliary public C API.
  src/server.c            - the single translation unit compiled; it #includes
                            the implementation .c files listed above.
  src/*.c                 - the implementation units server.c #includes.
  src/*.h                 - the internal headers they need (vect.h, no_asm.h,
                            consts.h, point.h, fields.h, ec_ops.h, ec_mult.h,
                            errors.h, bytes.h, sha256.h and the *-addchain.h
                            tables).
